commit.message:
Register debug tools in MCP server

Added four new MCP tools for WordPress plugin debug mode:
- check_debug_status - Check if debug mode is enabled
- enable_debug - Turn on debug logging
- read_debug_log - Read log with pagination/tail support
- disable_debug - Disable debug and delete log file

Changes:
- wordpress_manager.py: Added 4 debug functions matching REST API endpoints
- peeperfrog_create_server.py:
  - Imported debug functions
  - Registered 4 debug tools in list_tools
  - Added handlers in handle_tool_call

All tools require manage_options capability (Administrator)
Plugin version requirement: 1.4.0+

commit.message:
Users/VisitLog 연동 + 권한/프로젝트 규칙 + Project 내부 역할 개선

1) Admin 구성원 추가 시 deptname(소속그룹)/mail DB 저장 + 리스트 표시
2) visit_log middleware 추가 (GET /api/* 요청 자동 로깅)
3) Admin Role 3개 단순화 (super_admin/admin/member), AI Settings super_admin only
4) Private project: 멤버만 조회 가능 (admin도 비멤버면 안보임)
5) SUPER_ADMIN 하드코딩 완전 제거 (is_super_admin_user, require_admin)
6) Project 내부 역할(manager) 추가: 멤버 역할 변경 API + Settings UI
7) Task 조회: membership 기준으로 변경 (assignee 무관하게 멤버면 전체 task 노출)
8) require_admin → admin+super_admin 허용, require_super_admin 신규 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate nav menu and calendar event persistence

- Add [client] showSidebarNavigation=false to config.toml to hide
  Streamlit's auto-generated pages nav (fixes duplicate sidebar menu)
- Fix calendar event persistence: change list_events Supabase query
  from select("*, practices(name)") to select("*") — the PostgREST
  embedded select requires FK relationships registered in Supabase;
  when it fails the exception was silently swallowed in _gather_events,
  making the calendar appear empty after every save
- Replace silent except-pass in _gather_events with a visible warning
  so future query errors surface instead of hiding
- Update calendar CSS, event colors, day cells, and legend to match
  the light clinical theme (remove all hardcoded dark hex colors)

Fix add item: use correct field keys and schema for Slack Lists API

Three bugs in handleAddItem:
1. Title field used key "title" but Slack expects "name"
2. Status/assignee/client fields used column IDs as keys but
   items.create expects column keys; also used {id} format for
   select values but Slack expects array format [optionId]
3. People and client columns were looked up in listData.columns
   (board status groups) instead of listData.schema (actual columns)

commit.message:
Fix silent order update failures (Already Done, Mark Shipped, etc.)

Two root causes:
1. useUpdateSalesOrder double-filtered by org_id on top of RLS,
   causing 0-row silent failures when user's profile org didn't match
   the order's org (super_admin/vendor users).
2. No super_admin UPDATE/DELETE RLS policy on sales_orders — super
   admins could see all orders but couldn't modify them.

Fixes: remove redundant .eq('org_id') filter (RLS handles scoping),
add explicit error when update affects 0 rows, add super_admin
UPDATE + DELETE policies.

fix(scraper): auto-rename misnamed EasyEcom inventory file before DB populate

EasyEcom sometimes returns a CSV file with a .zip extension. This caused
two failures: (1) _store_wh_stock_to_db skipped parsing due to wrong suffix,
and (2) the populate step's file glob (**/*.csv, **/*.xlsx) couldn't find the
file, resulting in 'No files found' and zero rows imported.

Fix: detect real format from magic bytes in _extract_from_zip and rename
to .csv or .xlsx before returning, so both the in-scraper upsert and the
workflow's Populate DB fallback step always find the correct file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

setup_rocm.sh: fix for vast.ai containers (git clone HIP headers)

vast.ai bind-mounts NVIDIA drivers into containers, so any apt package
that installs driver files (libnvidia-compute, nvidia-persistenced)
crashes dpkg with "Invalid cross-device link". This affects both
hip-runtime-nvidia (depends on `cuda` metapackage) and nvidia-cuda-toolkit.

Fix: use cuda-toolkit-12-4 (toolkit only, no drivers) + git clone HIP
headers from ROCm/HIP and ROCm/hipother repos. Zero apt dependency on
nvidia driver packages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: invert broker avg cost scoring to bell-curve logic (v1.14)

Old logic: price below broker cost = STRONG_SELL (-2) ← backwards
New logic (bell curve):
  < -20%       → NEUTRAL  (0): deep underwater, may cut losses
  -20% to 0%   → STRONG_BUY (+2): defending — broker motivated to push up
  0% to +15%   → STRONG_BUY (+2): sweet spot — near cost, still incentivised
  +15% to +30% → BUY (+1): elevated, approaching take-profit range
  +30% to +50% → NEUTRAL (0): distribution starting
  > +50%       → SELL (-1): clear distribution zone

Reasoning: a broker underwater is MOST motivated to push price up.
A broker with huge unrealised gains is a distribution risk.
Also adds COST_DEEP_UNDERWATER = -20.0 constant to config.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(workflow-tools): add gate-keeper agent, /run-pep command, and Stop hook

Gate-keeper agent evaluates PEP wave completion with per-criterion
PASS/FAIL verdicts derived mechanically from evidence, not gestalt
scores. Encourages deterministic gates first (tests, type checks),
then judgement-based checks.

/run-pep reads a PEP, extracts the current wave, and generates a
headless claude -p command with two modes: single-shot (keeps context)
and fresh-context loop (restarts per attempt for complex waves).

Built-in Stop hook activates via .pep-gate state file. Zero overhead
on normal sessions. /run-pep creates the file, Claude deletes it when
gates pass. No manual settings configuration needed.

commit.message:
fix(sync): eliminate race conditions in pipeline tests (#454)

- Replace blind `tokio::time::sleep(100ms)` waits with deterministic block subscription checks using `handle.subscribe_blocks()` in 6 pipeline tests
- The sleep-based approach was flaky because `handle.stop()` could cancel in-progress work if the pipeline hadn't finished processing all chunks within the 100ms window (common under CI load or debug builds)
- The cancellation test (`run_should_be_cancelled_if_stop_requested`) is left unchanged since its sleep is intentional — it tests that `stop()` cancels a stage that never completes

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix CalibratedClassifierCV rare-class failure and DataFrame fragmentation

builder.py:
- Change cv=3 → cv=2 in CalibratedClassifierCV; sorted-ball datasets
  can have extreme ball positions (e.g. Ball6=6 or Ball1=44) that appear
  fewer than 3 times, causing a ValueError on fresh training

features.py:
- Add data.copy() before section 17 to defragment the DataFrame
  (avoids PerformanceWarning from many prior column inserts)
- Collect all sections 17-19 column values into a dict, then emit them
  in one pd.concat call instead of individual assignments; eliminates
  the repeated PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
